feedback.text: Please consider protecting video games from being destroyed Video games are "sold" to consumers, not rented, yet when they decide that those "sold" games are no longer producing enough money, the publishers tend to delist them from storefronts and even remove them from our digital libraries, which is literally theft . Please make it so they build an end-of-life plan from the development stage, so these video games don't disappear into the ether Books and movies getting lost to time is a tragedy, but these publishers say video games, which took 1000 /100.000 man-hours to build, are perfectly morally fine. Thank you for taking the time to read this.
